gaps: No existing test case covers the Custom Button settings page or its provider options at all.
No test case validates that the Custom Button setting refreshes its provider state (e.g., MS Teams vs. non-Teams) when the headset's connection to a PC changes.

No test case covers the lifecycle dimension: the bug requires an app restart to refresh the UI state. None of the existing tests validate that settings persist or refresh correctly across app background/foreground or app kill/relaunch cycles.

No test case covers the state trigger dimension: the bug occurs because the app does not re-sync the Custom Button provider state when the headset disconnects from a PC (a Bluetooth/audio routing event). Existing tests only check basic connection/disconnection of the headset to/from the phone, not multi-device connection scenarios where the headset is connected to both a phone and a PC simultaneously.

No test case validates device asymmetry: the Custom Button feature behavior may differ across headset models (e.g., Voyager 4220 UC vs. Voyager Focus 2 vs. Free 60). None of the existing tests validate Custom Button settings across different Poly headset models.

No test case covers the specific scenario of a headset being connected to a PC (via USB or Bluetooth) while also paired to the phone, and the app's UI reflecting the PC connection state in the Custom Button provider options.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_001: Provider state refresh on headset-PC disconnect (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Pair Voyager 4220 UC to phone AND connect to PC via USB Bluetooth adapter. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Custom Button Settings
Verify current provider shows 'MS Teams' (connected to PC).
Disconnect headset from PC (unplug USB adapter OR disconnect Bluetooth from PC).
Do NOT restart the app. Stay on Custom Button Settings page.
Observe the provider field for 10 seconds.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider updates to 'None' or 'No PC Connection' within 5 seconds of PC disconnect. Actual (If Bug): Provider still shows 'MS Teams' as connected until app is force-killed and relaunched.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_002: Provider state refresh on headset-PC reconnect (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 4220 UC paired to phone. Headset NOT connected to PC. App open on Custom Button Settings showing 'No PC Connection'.
Connect headset to PC (plug USB adapter or pair Bluetooth to PC).
Do NOT restart the app. Stay on Custom Button Settings page.
Observe the provider field for 10 seconds.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider updates to 'MS Teams' within 5 seconds of PC connection. Actual (If Bug): Provider still shows 'No PC Connection' until app is force-killed and relaunched.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_003: Immediate state trigger — kill app mid-PC disconnect (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 4220 UC connected to phone AND PC. App open on Custom Button Settings showing 'MS Teams'.
Disconnect headset from PC.
IMMEDIATELY (within 2 seconds) force-kill the app (swipe from recents).
Reopen the app.
Navigate to: Device → Custom Button Settings
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider shows 'No PC Connection' reflecting the PC-disconnected state. Actual (If Bug): Provider shows 'MS Teams' because the app was killed before the state sync completed, and no refresh occurs on relaunch.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_004: Deep lifecycle — app background/foreground after PC disconnect (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 4220 UC connected to phone AND PC. App open on Custom Button Settings showing 'MS Teams'.
Disconnect headset from PC.
Press Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds.
Bring app to foreground.
Observe Custom Button Settings provider field.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider updates to 'No PC Connection' after foregrounding. Actual (If Bug): Provider still shows 'MS Teams' — app does not re-sync on foreground lifecycle event.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_005: Device asymmetry — Voyager Focus 2 Custom Button provider refresh on PC disconnect
steps: Precondition: Pair Voyager Focus 2 to phone AND connect to PC via USB dongle. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Custom Button Settings
Verify current provider shows 'MS Teams' (connected to PC).
Disconnect headset from PC (unplug dongle).
Do NOT restart the app. Stay on Custom Button Settings page.
Observe the provider field for 10 seconds.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider updates to 'No PC Connection' within 5 seconds. Actual (If Bug): Provider still shows 'MS Teams' — Focus 2 exhibits same refresh failure as Voyager 4220 UC.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_006: Device asymmetry — Poly Free 60 Custom Button provider refresh on PC disconnect
steps: Precondition: Pair Poly Free 60 to phone AND connect to PC via Bluetooth. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Custom Button Settings
Verify current provider shows 'MS Teams' (connected to PC).
Disconnect headset from PC (disable Bluetooth on PC or disconnect device).
Do NOT restart the app. Stay on Custom Button Settings page.
Observe the provider field for 10 seconds.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider updates to 'No PC Connection' within 5 seconds. Actual (If Bug): Provider still shows 'MS Teams' — Free 60 exhibits same refresh failure as Voyager 4220 UC.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_007: Custom Button action options reflect correct provider state after PC disconnect (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 4220 UC connected to phone AND PC. App open on Custom Button Settings showing 'MS Teams'.
Note the available action options (e.g., 'Mute Microsoft Teams', 'Push-to-Talk', etc.).
Disconnect headset from PC.
Wait 10 seconds without restarting app.
Observe the available action options in the Custom Button settings.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: MS Teams-specific actions (e.g., 'Mute Microsoft Teams', 'Push-to-Talk') are removed/hidden, replaced by generic actions (e.g., 'Mute', 'Answer/End Call'). Actual (If Bug): MS Teams-specific actions remain visible despite PC disconnection.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_008: Custom Button setting persists across app restart after PC disconnect (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 4220 UC connected to phone AND PC. Custom Button set to 'Mute Microsoft Teams'.
Disconnect headset from PC.
Force-kill the app (swipe from recents).
Reopen the app.
Navigate to: Device → Custom Button Settings
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Custom Button action shows 'Mute Microsoft Teams' (user's last selection persists). Provider field shows 'No PC Connection'. Actual (If Bug): Custom Button action resets to default or provider incorrectly shows 'MS Teams'.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_009: Multi-device — headset connected to phone + PC, then PC disconnects, then phone Bluetooth toggles (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 4220 UC connected to phone AND PC. App open on Custom Button Settings showing 'MS Teams'.
Disconnect headset from PC.
Verify provider updates to 'No PC Connection' (wait up to 10 seconds).
Toggle phone Bluetooth OFF, then ON.
Wait for headset to reconnect to phone.
Observe Custom Button Settings provider field.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider remains 'No PC Connection' after Bluetooth reconnect (PC is still disconnected). Actual (If Bug): Provider incorrectly reverts to 'MS Teams' after Bluetooth toggle, or shows stale state.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_BUTTON_010: Custom Button provider state on app launch with headset already disconnected from PC (Voyager 4220 UC)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 4220 UC connected to phone. Headset NOT connected to PC. App is closed (force-killed).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Custom Button Settings
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Provider shows 'No PC Connection' immediately on first load. Actual (If Bug): Provider shows 'MS Teams' or loading state that never resolves to correct disconnected state.
